title: Technical Webinar on “Miniature Microsystems for Space Applications”
content: Event Date and Year: 23rd August 2024, 3:00 PM, Online Mode Event Venue: Online (google meet) https://meet.google.com/rkx-gghp-skq Event Brief Description – On the occasion of National Space Day 2024, the IEEE IAS Chapter at Galgotias University, in collaboration with the Department of Electrical, Electronics & Communication Engineering (DEECE), organized a highly insightful webinar on the theme “Miniature Microsystems for Space Applications.” The session was delivered by Dr. Deepak Kumar Sharma, an eminent academician and former ISRO Scientist, PEC Chandigarh, AIR 1 – ISRO Exam. The webinar aimed to celebrate India’s remarkable journey in the space sector and inspire young minds to explore careers in aerospace research and technology. Participation and Event Overview: The webinar attracted a wide audience of students, faculty, and research enthusiasts who were keen to learn about the advancements in space technology. Dr. Sharma explained how miniature microsystems are enabling compact, reliable, and energy-efficient solutions for spacecraft, satellites, and aerospace applications. Event Structure: The webinar began with a welcome address by the Chair of IEEE IAS Chapter, who emphasized the significance of National Space Day and India’s proud achievements in the global space sector. Dr. Sharma’s technical session followed, where he shared insights into: Event Detailed Description The IEEE IAS Chapter at Galgotias University proudly organized a technical webinar on the occasion of National Space Day 2024, focusing on the theme “Miniature Microsystems for Space Applications.” Supported by the Department of Electrical, Electronics & Communication Engineering (DEECE), this event highlighted the transformative role of miniature and MEMS (Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems) technologies in space research and exploration. The session was conducted by Dr. Deepak Kumar Sharma, a distinguished academician and former ISRO scientist, who brought in-depth knowledge and real-world experience from India’s space programs. The talk covered various aspects including MEMS sensors, microsystems integration, space-grade electronics, and their critical applications in navigation, communication, and payload monitoring. Participants were able to connect theoretical concepts with practical applications in space systems, making the session highly impactful. The session concluded with an interactive Q&A segment, where participants clarified their queries regarding research opportunities, ISRO’s current projects, and global trends in space technology. Department Name – Department of Electrical, Electronics & Communication Engineering Event Outcome: The National Space Day Webinar on “Miniature Microsystems for Space Applications” proved to be a thought-provoking and inspiring event. Participants gained valuable insights into the significance of MEMS and miniature systems in space missions, which are vital for making spacecraft smaller, more efficient, and cost-effective. The event not only celebrated India’s achievements in the space sector but also motivated students to explore career opportunities in aerospace research, satellite technology, and advanced electronics. By bridging theoretical knowledge with real-world applications, the webinar fostered awareness about India’s growing presence in the global space ecosystem.
